sections.heading: Systemic
sections.content: Systemic therapy for vulvar cancer is extrapolated from cervical cancer and other SCCs due to rarity of disease. Options include chemotherapy, immunotherapy, and targeted therapy based on biomarkers.

cards.name: Cisplatin (concurrent with RT)
cards.detail: Setting: Locally advanced, concurrent chemoradiation. Drugs: Cisplatin 40 mg/m2 IV weekly during RT. Cycle: Weekly for 5–6 cycles. Toxicities: Nephrotoxicity, nausea, vomiting, ototoxicity, neuropathy.

cards.name: Carboplatin (concurrent with RT)
cards.detail: Setting: Locally advanced, concurrent chemoradiation (if cisplatin intolerant). Drugs: Carboplatin AUC 2 IV weekly during RT. Cycle: Weekly for 5–6 cycles. Toxicities: Myelosuppression, nephrotoxicity, nausea.

cards.name: Cisplatin/paclitaxel ± pembrolizumab ± bevacizumab
cards.detail: Setting: First-line for advanced/recurrent/metastatic disease. Drugs: Cisplatin 50 mg/m2 IV day 1, Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 IV day 1, Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV day 1, Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg IV day 1. Cycle: Every 21 days. Toxicities: Neuropathy, myelosuppression, fatigue, hypertension, proteinuria, immune-related adverse events.

cards.name: Pembrolizumab monotherapy (second-line for PD-L1+)
cards.detail: Setting: Second-line for advanced/recurrent/metastatic disease with PD-L1 CPS ≥1. Drugs: Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV day 1. Cycle: Every 21 days. Toxicities: Immune-related adverse events (colitis, hepatitis, pneumonitis, endocrinopathies).

cards.name: Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki (second-line for HER2+)
cards.detail: Setting: Second-line for advanced/recurrent/metastatic disease with HER2 IHC 3+ or 2+. Drugs: Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki 5.4 mg/kg IV day 1. Cycle: Every 21 days. Toxicities: Interstitial lung disease, myelosuppression, nausea, fatigue.

cards.name: Ipilimumab + nivolumab
cards.detail: Setting: Second-line for advanced/recurrent/metastatic disease. Drugs: Ipilimumab 1 mg/kg IV day 1, Nivolumab 240 mg IV day 1. Cycle: Every 21 days for 4 cycles, then nivolumab maintenance. Toxicities: Immune-related adverse events (colitis, hepatitis, pneumonitis, endocrinopathies).

timeline.title: Concurrent Chemoradiation (Locally Advanced)
timeline.body: Preferred: Cisplatin. Rationale: Standard radiosensitizer with extensive evidence in cervical cancer. Alternatives: Carboplatin if cisplatin intolerant, Cisplatin/fluorouracil, Capecitabine/mitomycin (if cisplatin/carboplatin unavailable), Gemcitabine, Paclitaxel. Biomarker requirements: None.

timeline.title: 1st Line (Advanced/Metastatic)
timeline.body: Preferred: Cisplatin/paclitaxel + pembrolizumab ± bevacizumab. Rationale: Based on KEYNOTE-826 trial in cervical cancer. Alternatives: Carboplatin/paclitaxel + pembrolizumab ± bevacizumab, Cisplatin/paclitaxel ± bevacizumab, Carboplatin/paclitaxel ± bevacizumab, Cisplatin/paclitaxel, Carboplatin/paclitaxel, Single-agent cisplatin or carboplatin. Biomarker requirements: PD-L1 testing may inform pembrolizumab use; TMB-H, MSI-H/dMMR, HER2, NTRK for subsequent therapy.

timeline.title: 2nd Line (Advanced/Metastatic)
timeline.body: Preferred: Paclitaxel. Rationale: Modest activity in phase II trial. Alternatives: Cemiplimab, Erlotinib (category 2B), Cisplatin/gemcitabine (category 2B), Ipilimumab + nivolumab, Biomarker-directed: pembrolizumab for TMB-H, PD-L1+, MSI-H/dMMR; fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki for HER2+; nivolumab for HPV-related; larotrectinib, entrectinib, repotrectinib for NTRK fusion-positive. Biomarker requirements: PD-L1, TMB, MSI/MMR, HER2, HPV, NTRK testing recommended for biomarker-directed options.
